jokes.text: Well, you're certainly doing your job today, Mr. Sun.
jokes.setup: Extreme heat wave making everyone miserable
jokes.punchline: Homer addresses the sun like it's an overzealous employee
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer observation - treating nature like a person he can have a workplace relationship with

jokes.text: I hardly think it's fair to be charged full price. I'm up to my knees in the original cast of M*A*S*H.
jokes.setup: Wax museum figures melting in the heat
jokes.punchline: Customer complaint about paying full price when attraction is literally melting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy - the specific reference to M*A*S*H cast makes it funnier than generic 'celebrities'

jokes.text: Homer, my hat goes off to you. - It's cool in here, boy.
jokes.setup: Someone praising Homer for his ingenuity
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the metaphor, thinks they're literally commenting on temperature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his literal-mindedness preventing him from understanding praise

jokes.text: For the rest of the summer, we can live inside the refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Homer found a solution to the heat
jokes.punchline: His solution is to literally live in the fridge
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - taking a practical observation to its most impractical extreme

jokes.text: I got the idea when I noticed the refrigerator was cold.
jokes.setup: Marge asking how Homer came up with his brilliant cooling solution
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'eureka moment' was noticing the most obvious property of a refrigerator
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's proud presentation of the most obvious observation as genius

jokes.text: Marge, can you set the oven to cold?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to expand his cooling strategy
jokes.punchline: Asking to set an oven to cold - fundamentally misunderstanding how appliances work
jokes.explanation: Homer's logical extension of his refrigerator discovery shows his complete misunderstanding of basic physics

jokes.text: I'm all out of ice cream! It's true, you know. Ice cream! Ice cream! Fresh out of ice cream!
jokes.setup: Ice cream vendor in extreme heat
jokes.punchline: Vendor desperately trying to convince people he really is out of ice cream on the hottest day
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony - the one day everyone wants ice cream is when he can't keep it cold

jokes.text: Chili! Red-hot, Texas-style chili! And we got ginger ale. Boiling hot, Texas-style ginger ale!
jokes.setup: Food vendor in extreme heat trying to sell the worst possible products
jokes.punchline: Adding 'Texas-style' and 'boiling hot' to make the products sound even less appealing
jokes.explanation: Escalates the ice cream joke perfectly - vendor doubling down on the worst possible heat-wave products

jokes.text: Face it, Bart. Our salvation isn't gonna roll by on the back of some stupid truck.
jokes.setup: Lisa telling Bart they need to be realistic about their situation
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by the pool-mobile arriving
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate contradiction - Lisa's pragmatism instantly proven wrong by perfect timing

jokes.text: Pool-mobile? Pool-mobile?
jokes.setup: Lisa just said salvation won't come via truck
jokes.punchline: Kids' desperate, disbelieving excitement at perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the ironic timing setup. Their repeated question shows disbelief at their luck.

jokes.text: Look, Lis, I snatched five bathing suits. All Martin's.
jokes.setup: Bart preparing for pool time
jokes.punchline: All the suits he grabbed belong to Martin, the nerdiest kid
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - of course the only suits available would be from the least athletic kid

jokes.text: Take your best shot. I'm wearing 17 layers.
jokes.setup: Martin preparing for potential pool conflict
jokes.punchline: His defense strategy is to wear an absurd number of bathing suits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin moment - overthinking protection with completely impractical solution

jokes.text: I'm gonna stow away underwater and go where the pool goes. Have a good life.
jokes.setup: Pool time ending, Bart desperate not to leave
jokes.punchline: His plan is to hide underwater and live wherever the mobile pool goes
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart impulsiveness - dramatic farewell combined with completely unworkable escape plan

jokes.text: No way, man. One day of summer's all we're budgeted for.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if the pool-mobile will return tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Otto reveals the harsh economic reality behind their brief joy
jokes.explanation: Otto's casual delivery of crushing news fits his character perfectly - bureaucratic reality crushing childhood dreams

jokes.text: I guess it is kind of a tease.
jokes.setup: Otto explaining why they only provide one day of pool access
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about giving kids one perfect day then taking it away forever
jokes.explanation: Otto's stoned understatement of what's actually psychological torture for children

jokes.text: Let us celebrate our new arrangement with the adding of chocolate to milk.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to buy a pool
jokes.punchline: His formal, ceremonial way of saying 'let's have chocolate milk'
jokes.explanation: Homer's pompous phrasing of the most mundane celebration perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Over here we have the Hick Tub, the Insta-Rust. That's the Lightning Magnet. That's the Tinkler.
jokes.setup: Salesman showing different pool models
jokes.punchline: Each name is progressively worse - ending with 'Tinkler'
jokes.explanation: Escalating series of terrible product names, each worse than the last

jokes.text: The Tinkler. I like the sound of that. We'll take it.
jokes.setup: Pool salesman mentions the worst-named pool
jokes.punchline: Homer is immediately sold by the name that sounds like urination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer decision-making - choosing based on immature name association rather than quality

jokes.text: Is it true we should wait at least an hour after eating before we go in?
jokes.setup: Homer just bought a pool called 'The Tinkler'
jokes.punchline: Now he's concerned about swimming safety myths
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Homer worried about minor safety myth after buying obviously dangerous pool. Character-specific misplaced priorities.

jokes.text: Look, question lady, this job is not what I really do, okay? I play keyboards.
jokes.setup: Marge asking practical pool safety questions
jokes.punchline: Salesman dismissing her concerns because he's really a musician
jokes.explanation: Captures the stereotype of service workers who think they're above their jobs

jokes.text: Look, question lady, this job is not what I really do, okay? I play keyboards.
jokes.setup: Homer asking reasonable pool safety question
jokes.punchline: Salesman's defensive artistic pretensions and dismissive attitude
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - the struggling artist/retail worker dynamic. 'Question lady' is particularly dismissive and funny.

jokes.text: 'Tis a fine barn, but sure 'tis no pool, English.
jokes.setup: Pool installation going wrong
jokes.punchline: Willie's Scottish accent delivering a variation of his famous barn line
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that works perfectly in context - Willie's signature catchphrase adapted for the situation

jokes.text: Is your pool ready yet?
jokes.setup: Kids arriving the instant the Simpsons get a pool
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's transparent eagerness and poor timing
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse lack of social awareness - arriving before the pool is even filled

jokes.text: Friends and well-wishers, yes.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if all the kids are Bart's friends
jokes.punchline: Bart's diplomatic way of acknowledging these are opportunistic relationships
jokes.explanation: Bart's sophisticated understanding of his sudden popularity - acknowledging the transactional nature

jokes.text: Hey, Bart! Your epidermis is showing!
jokes.setup: Nelson trying to embarrass Bart in front of everyone
jokes.punchline: Using a fancy word for skin to make it sound dirty
jokes.explanation: Classic playground 'trick' - using scientific terminology to sound more sophisticated than it is

jokes.text: See, epidermis means your hair. So technically it's true. That's what makes it so funny.
jokes.setup: Nelson explaining his 'clever' prank
jokes.punchline: He's wrong about what epidermis means, making his explanation even funnier
jokes.explanation: Double layer joke - Nelson's confident explanation is scientifically wrong, making him look even more foolish

jokes.text: I'm sorry, that leg's gonna have to come off.
jokes.setup: Bart injured and at the hospital
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually suggesting amputation
jokes.explanation: Dr. Hibbert's casual attitude toward extreme medical procedures

jokes.text: Did I say leg? I meant that wet bathing suit.
jokes.setup: Just threatened to amputate Bart's leg
jokes.punchline: Casual correction that he just meant remove the wet swimsuit
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - from terrifying to mundane with Hibbert's casual delivery

jokes.text: Don't worry, boy. When you get a job like me, you'll miss every summer.
jokes.setup: Bart worried about missing summer
jokes.punchline: Homer's bleak adult perspective delivered as comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Homer wisdom - trying to comfort by sharing adult misery. Captures working-class resignation beautifully.

jokes.text: Don't worry, boy. When you get a job like me, you'll miss every summer.
jokes.setup: Bart worried about missing summer due to his injury
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'comforting' perspective that adult life is even worse
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at comfort is actually depressing wisdom about adult life - perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Isn't it amazing, the same day you got a pool is the same day we realized we liked you?
jokes.setup: Kids visiting Bart in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Brazenly admitting their friendship is entirely transactional
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest acknowledgment of fake friendship - the timing comment makes it even more cutting

jokes.text: Faithful Milhouse. You'll spend the long, hot days by my side, won't you?
jokes.setup: All other friends abandoned Bart for the pool
jokes.punchline: Bart's melodramatic appeal to his loyal friend
jokes.explanation: Sets up Milhouse's betrayal. Bart's overly dramatic language shows his desperation.

jokes.text: I think I lost my glasses in your pool. I better go in and find them. - But you're wearing your glasses. - No, I'm not.
jokes.setup: Milhouse making excuse to use the pool
jokes.punchline: His obvious lie about losing glasses while wearing them, then doubling down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - transparent lie followed by doubling down when caught, shows his desperation and social awkwardness

jokes.text: But you're wearing your glasses. - No, I'm not.
jokes.setup: Milhouse claiming he lost his glasses
jokes.punchline: His absurd denial of obvious reality to maintain excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Milhouse's desperation. His flat denial of visible reality is beautifully absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, look. Bart's all alone. Maybe I should keep him company, at least for a little while.
jokes.setup: Lisa notices Bart's abandonment
jokes.punchline: Her genuine sympathy immediately tested by pool temptation
jokes.explanation: Setup for Lisa's temptation. Shows her better nature but plants seeds of conflict.

jokes.text: Great. I get to spend the summer with my brain.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes he's alone and has to think/read
jokes.punchline: Treating his own intelligence as unwanted company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant way of expressing Bart's anti-intellectual attitude - personifying his brain as an annoying companion

jokes.text: What fun can you have in a pool anyway that you can't have in a bathtub with a garbage bag taped around your cast?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to cope with missing pool fun
jokes.punchline: His elaborate rationalization about bathtub equivalency
jokes.explanation: Good rationalization comedy - Bart's detailed but obviously inadequate substitute plan. The garbage bag detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hope you enjoyed that, kids, because Krusty's out of here for the summer. In the meantime, we'll be running classic Krusty. Enjoy!
jokes.setup: Kids looking forward to summer TV
jokes.punchline: Their favorite show is going on hiatus with terrible reruns
jokes.explanation: Relatable summer disappointment - favorite shows going into reruns just when kids have time to watch

jokes.text: Good evening. Tonight my guest is AFL-CIO Chairman George Meany...who will be discussing collective-bargaining agreements.
jokes.setup: Classic Krusty show supposedly for kids
jokes.punchline: Featuring the most boring adult topic imaginable
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of how terrible 'classic' reruns are - children's show featuring union negotiations

jokes.text: Lisa, the blob has got me! Don't touch me or it'll get you too!
jokes.setup: Pool maintenance being neglected
jokes.punchline: Homer covered in algae, treating it like monster movie
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy. Homer's B-movie reaction to natural consequences of his laziness is very in character.

jokes.text: Dad, you have to put chlorine in the water every day to keep it clean. - Chlorine, eh?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining pool maintenance to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's intrigued 'eh?' suggesting he'll misuse this information
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation that Homer will misunderstand or overdo the chlorine application

jokes.text: My face is on fire!
jokes.setup: Homer learning about chlorine
jokes.punchline: Immediately overdoing it and burning himself
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the chlorine setup - Homer's inevitable overuse of chemicals with immediate consequences

jokes.text: I swear I don't know where the bombs are! Only the miners know that! Maybe this will loosen your tongue. No!
jokes.setup: Bart writing a play to occupy his time
jokes.punchline: His 'play' is a violent interrogation scene
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's creative interests lean toward action and violence - inappropriate content for a kid's play

jokes.text: Cast of characters: Viceroy Fizzlebottom, a hearty cherub of a man...
jokes.setup: Lisa reading Bart's play
jokes.punchline: Bart's flowery, overly literary character descriptions
jokes.explanation: Contrast between Bart's violent content and pretentiously literary style shows his creative confusion

jokes.text: Bart, it's the best thing that ever happened to me in my whole life!
jokes.setup: Bart asking how Lisa likes being popular
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overwhelming enthusiasm contradicts her attempt to be modest
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Lisa's intellectual honesty overcomes her attempt at diplomacy. Shows her genuine excitement.

jokes.text: There was an Optics Festival and I wasn't informed? You go now.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning she won the telescope at an Optics Festival
jokes.punchline: Bart's outrage at missing such a nerdy event, dismissing Lisa
jokes.explanation: Bart's boredom has reached the point where even the nerdiest activities sound appealing

jokes.text: What have we here? 'The Lighter Side of Hippies.' They don't care whose toes they step on.
jokes.setup: Bart finding reading material
jokes.punchline: Magazine about hippies described as stepping on toes - opposite of hippie philosophy
jokes.explanation: Ironic misreading of hippie culture as aggressive rather than peaceful

jokes.text: They don't care whose toes they step on.
jokes.setup: Bart reading hippie humor magazine
jokes.punchline: Mild pun about hippie footwear/behavior delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Mild pun about hippies going barefoot. Shows the magazine's gentle humor style. Not particularly strong but fits.

jokes.text: Do not be alarmed. Continue swimming naked.
jokes.setup: Bart spying on neighbors with telescope
jokes.punchline: Encouraging inappropriate behavior while voyeuristically watching
jokes.explanation: Bart's descent into creepy behavior due to boredom and isolation

jokes.text: All right, Lou, open fire.
jokes.setup: Police responding to peeping Tom
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to lethal force
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum incompetence - massive overreaction to minor crime

jokes.text: Wow, the universe is so boring.
jokes.setup: Bart looking at the night sky through telescope
jokes.punchline: Finding the entire universe boring - the ultimate in jadedness
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Bart's bored state - even the cosmos can't interest him

jokes.text: Wow, the universe is so boring.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to find entertainment through telescope
jokes.punchline: His dismissal of cosmic wonder as dull
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - finding infinite cosmic mystery boring. Great contrast between scope and attention span.

jokes.text: I know. I'll use this to peer into Springfield's seamy underbelly.
jokes.setup: Bart bored with universe, wants entertainment
jokes.punchline: His decision to spy on neighbors with dramatic language
jokes.explanation: 'Seamy underbelly' is perfectly overwrought for suburban spying. Sets up the murder mystery plot nicely.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! What have I done? I've killed her!
jokes.setup: Bart witnessing what appears to be Ned confessing to murder
jokes.punchline: Ned's dramatic confession sets up the mystery plot
jokes.explanation: Strong plot turn that subverts expectations about perfect neighbor Ned

jokes.text: I wish there was some other explanation for this, but there isn't! I'm a murderer! I'm a murderer!
jokes.setup: Ned continuing his apparent confession
jokes.punchline: His anguished repetition increases Bart's certainty
jokes.explanation: Escalates the false confession while maintaining the misdirection

jokes.text: I'm a mur-diddly-erdiler!
jokes.setup: Ned confessing to murder
jokes.punchline: Even in crisis, he maintains his signature speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ned's speech tic persists even during emotional breakdown, making it both funny and disturbing

jokes.text: If that's not Flanders, he's done his homework.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to rationalize what he heard
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging that the distinctive speech pattern proves it's really Ned
jokes.explanation: Bart's logical assessment of the evidence - the speech pattern is too distinctive to fake

jokes.text: That's horrible...in principle.
jokes.setup: Bart saying Ned will kill his kids too
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectually honest but emotionally detached response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - her analytical nature makes her response inappropriately academic for the situation

jokes.text: They're only using you for your pool, you know. - Shut up, brain. I got friends now. I don't need you anymore.
jokes.setup: Lisa's conscience warning her about fair-weather friends
jokes.punchline: She tells her own brain to shut up because she enjoys being popular
jokes.explanation: Brilliant internal dialogue showing Lisa's intellectual honesty in conflict with her desire for acceptance

jokes.text: My plan has come to fruition. Soon I'll be queen of summertime. Oh, king. King!
jokes.setup: Lisa enjoying her pool-based popularity
jokes.punchline: Her grandiose plans and immediate gender correction
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's ambitious nature and quick self-correction about gender roles

jokes.text: Listen, Ned Flanders murdered his wife! But why? She's such a fox. I mean, what's on Fox tonight? Something ribald, no doubt.
jokes.setup: Bart urgently telling Homer about murder
jokes.punchline: Homer's distraction by TV network name leads to commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer attention span problem with meta-humor about Fox programming. Good misdirection from serious topic.

jokes.text: But why? She's such a fox. I mean, what's on Fox tonight? Something ribald, no doubt.
jokes.setup: Homer reacting to news about Maude Flanders
jokes.punchline: Catches himself using 'fox' about neighbor's wife, redirects to TV network
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer save - catching his inappropriate comment and redirecting with wordplay that maintains character

jokes.text: Kippers for breakfast, Aunt Helga? Is it Saint Swiven's Day already? 'Tis, replied Aunt Helga...
jokes.setup: Bart reading from his pretentious play
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate dialogue about mundane breakfast topic
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's attempt at high literature resulting in pompous nonsense

jokes.text: If you know the name of the felony being committed, press one. To choose from a list of felonies, press two.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to call police for emergency
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic phone tree for life-threatening situations
jokes.explanation: Satirizes automated phone systems that prioritize organization over urgency

jokes.text: If you are being murdered or calling from a rotary phone...please stay on the line.
jokes.setup: Police phone system continuing its categories
jokes.punchline: Equating murder with outdated phone technology as equal inconveniences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation treating murder and rotary phones as equally antiquated problems

jokes.text: You have selected regicide. If you know the name of the king or queen being murdered, press one.
jokes.setup: Phone system getting more specific about murder types
jokes.punchline: Regicide option with even more specific subcategories
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd bureaucracy by getting even more specific about rare crime types

jokes.text: That's right. I was at Bible camp. I was learning how to be more judgmental.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining where Maude really is
jokes.punchline: Bible camp teaching the opposite of Christian values
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of religious hypocrisy - Bible camp teaching un-Christian behavior with Ned's earnest delivery

jokes.text: I over-watered Maude's favorite ficus plant...I panicked, and then I buried the remains.
jokes.setup: Ned's dramatic confession to murder
jokes.punchline: He 'murdered' and 'buried' a houseplant
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the murder mystery - Ned's guilt over killing a plant with the same dramatic language as human murder

jokes.text: Really, I mean that. Sorry if it sounded sarcastic.
jokes.setup: Wiggum saying everything is wrapped up neatly
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his own tendency toward sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's rare moment of self-awareness about his communication style
